subject: Databases
title: Choosing the Right Level
description: A 60-second lesson on Choosing the Right Level.

hook.text: Read Committed for most queries. Repeatable Read for reporting. Serializable for money. Match the level to the need.

buildup.text: Higher isolation costs more: more locks, more aborts, less throughput. Don't over-isolate.

discovery.text: You can set isolation per transaction: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE. Only critical paths pay the cost.

twist.text: Many developers never change the default. They inherit Read Committed and discover anomalies in production.

climax.text: Understand your anomalies first, then pick the level that prevents them. Don't guess.

punchline.text: Match the isolation level to the anomaly you need to prevent.

quiz.question: Why should you set isolation level per transaction instead of globally?
options: Only critical transactions need high isolation; others can run with less overhead
Global settings are not supported by any database
Per-transaction settings are faster in every case
